Coarse grains of slightly lineated hornblende euhedra with anhedra of alkali feldspar make up the bulk of this rock. There are scattered grains of pyrite and possibly a trace of quartz as well. The modes were 69% hornblende, 30.4% feldspar and 0.6% opaques. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss6.spectrum.txt

Very large augen of microcline enclose smaller albite and orthoclase euhedra along with anhedra of quartz and biotite euhedra. There is minor sericitization of the feldspars, scattered opaques (magnetite?) and some myrmeketic texture is present. There are much lesser amounts of anhedral green amphibole in this rock along with trace amounts of zircon euhedra. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.coarse.gneiss8.spectrum.txt

This coarse to very coarse-grained sample consists of anhedra of quartz, alkali feldspar (albite, orthoclase, microcline) and brown and green biotite. Some regions are heavily sericitized, and sericitization has also occurred along most feldspar grain boundaries and cracks. There are scattered opaques and some myrmeketic texture in this sample along with a trace of zircon. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss3.spectrum.txt

This sample probably represents a metamorphosed zircon syenite. It is a coarse-grained sample showing granulation along most grain boundaries. There are abundant anhedra of nepheline and microperthitic feldspar, an amphibole (either hornblende or a sodic phase), abundant magnetite and numerous grains of zircon. Cancrinite occurs aroundfeldspars scattered throughout the section. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss4.spectrum.txt

This coarse-grained rock has been sheared enough to giveit mortar texture. The abundant albite subhedra (some with perthite) often evidence brittle deformation, while other areas have recrystallized. In addition to this phase there are muscoviteeuhedra (secondary to deformation), and larger primary muscovite heavily sheared to sericite in foliated masses or bands throughout the sample. Modes were 67% albite and 33% muscovite. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.gneiss.fine.gneiss5.spectrum.txt

This rock consists of a fine-grained to microcrystalline groundmass with a pleochroic brown phase (biotite?), opaques, cordierite and possibly some feldpsar. Large porphyroblasts of cordierite with heavy concentrations of tiny sillimanite needles are rimmed with cordierite and opaque granules.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.hornfels.fine.hornfe3.spectrum.txt

A medium-grained rock containing anhedral and some euhedral dolomite. There are scattered anhedra and subhedra of monticellite, subhedral phlogopite and some rounded quartz grains present. These latter minerals often contain opaque inclusions, opaques (some at least of which are pyrite) are also disseminated throughout the entire sample. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble1.spectrum.txt

This sample consists of coarse-grained anhedral carbonate with numerous regions of serpentine (possibly brucite--MgOH2, at least in part) as an alteration/reaction of the carbonate. There are scattered subhedra of monticellite, tremolite, and phlogopite, with a few grains of idocrase (?) also noted in this section.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble2.spectrum.txt

This is a medium-grained sample containing anhedral calcite with a few scattered (small) regions of recrystallized quartz.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble3.spectrum.txt

A fine-grained rock with granulated dolomite and a trace of tremolite/actinolite, and possibly some phlogopite.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble4.spectrum.txt

A medium grained sample of anhedral calcite heavily altered to serpentine. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble5.spectrum.txt

This rock contains coarse-grained carbonate with scattered small subrounded quartz grains and minor subhedral tremolite which has altered to talc in a few spots.Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble6.spectrum.txt

This sample consists of coarse-grained calcite poikiloblastically enclosing a finely disseminated opaque/ferric phase in the cores of the carbonate. There has been a considerable amount of granulation (cataclasis) with some carbonate grains showing bent lamellae. There are trace amounts of larger opaque phases in this rock. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.marble.fine.marble7.spectrum.txt

This is a dark gray fine-grained to very fine-grained sample consisting of typical crenulated muscovite and quartz. Opaques, which usually have been rotated, occur throughout the sample -- pyrite, and either magnetite or hematite. There appear to be some carbonaceous granules scattered throughout the section as well. Modes were 65.9% muscovite, 33.5% quartz, 0.5% opaque and a trace of carbonaceous material. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.phyllite.fine.phylli1.spectrum.txt

A schistose sample consisting of medium to coarse-grained recrystallized quartz with some muscovite that often contains ferruginous material. In addition there are scattered opaques present in this sample. The modal analyses for this rock are 88.2% quartz, 7.2% muscovite, 3.5% opaques and 1.1% other. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit4.spectrum.txt

This is a sample much like the previous one in composition. It is a fine-grained slightly metamorphosed well-compacted sandstone with some secondary quartz deposition, and much more silica/chalcedony deposition. The quartz grains, unlike the previous rock, show evidence of only weak strain and fewer fluid inclusions. In addition there are scattered detrital green and brown tourmaline and zircon. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit5.spectrum.txt

A well-compacted sample with medium-grained subangular to subrounded quartz grains and small amounts of interstitial calcite cement. There is also a trace amount of secondary quartz cement Fluid inclusions are common in the quartz with some grains showing strain, and trace amounts of feldspar are present. The calcite cement itself is stained with ferruginous material and some clay. Modal analyses gave 69% quartz, 25% calcite, 5% clay, 1% feldspar. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit1.spectrum.txt

This medium to fine-grained rock contains aligned quartz grains showing strain, pressure solution effects, recrystallization and scattered fluid inclusions. Green bands or lenses contain cores of larger grains surrounded by smaller ones produced by mechanical breakage (cataclasis). These grains are colorless to very pale green and appear to be tremolite. Modes for this sample were 77.5% quartz and 22.5% amphibole. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.quartzite.fine.qrtzit2.spectrum.txt

This is a medium to coarse-grained sample containing tremolite-actinolite crystals and talc. Amphibole is heavily altered to talc, and in addition to these phases there is quartz and some carbonate, as an alteration product, along with opaque grains. There may be heavily altered plagioclase present in this sample as well. Modes were 50.6% talc, 38% amphibole, 5.4% quartz, 3.6% calcite, 2.4% plagioclase remnants. Particle size was 500-1500 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.coarse.schist1.spectrum.txt

This rock contains coarse, irregular bands of brown, tan and gray material. Microscopically there are finer grains of calcite surrounded and enmeshed in a network of graphite bands and veins. There are also larger recrystallized calcite grains which are not associated with the graphite. Modes for this rock gave 62.5% calcite and 37.5% graphite. Particle size was 0-75 Micrometer. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.metamorphic.schist.fine.schist10.spectrum.txt
